description: The 501c4 political arm run by Tyler Bowyer; Chase the Vote budget jump and the disputed political-spending-reporting question. Disputed by TPUSA.

title: America's Turning Point (501c3)
description: The 501c3 sibling run by Tyler Bowyer funded by TPUSA transfers; the 8.6M grant and ~80 unreported employees claim. Disputed by TPUSA.

title: Turning Point Endowment (501c3)
description: The 501c3 holding Turning Point investments; the missing-990s claim and TPUSA rebuttal that all 990s were filed. Disputed.

title: Turning Point PAC
description: Turning Point's political action committee the entity fully permitted to do political activity; its role in the reporting debate. Attributed.

title: T.W. Lewis Foundation
description: A conservative donor foundation that reportedly lists TPUSA as a key partner and funds it; donor-network context. Attributed public claims.

title: Faith and Family Foundation Charity (Twiggs)
description: A Texas nonprofit linked only by a Twiggs surname match; logged for verification. Name-match only no established connection.

title: Heritage Foundation (Context)
description: The think tank that recurs at the edges of the case via an internship a Tucker Carlson donor dispute and shared TPUSA donors. Context not accusation.

title: Moore DM Group / Resource One (Claims)
description: A political direct-mail company said to be the real entity behind TPUSA Resource One printing line alleging a gap between 9M paid and 1.35M reported. Disputed.

title: Superfeed Technologies (Claims)
description: A tech company whose board reportedly includes Lori Frantzve and Tyler Bowyer with alleged TP Action fund flow. Attributed claims not proven findings.

title: Baker Tilly — TPUSA's Auditor (Claims)
description: The accounting firm reported to perform TPUSA annual audit one concluding June 20 2025 weeks before Kirk's death. Attributed disputed interpretation.

title: Salem Media (Claims)
description: The media company that carried the Kirk show and whose properties a Parscale FARA filing reportedly targeted for Israeli-government messaging. Attributed.

title: Boston Consulting Group (BCG) — Context (Claims)
description: The consulting firm that recurs via an intelligence official on a scrutinized flight and reported Gaza work. Attributed context not accusation.

title: Narrative
description: Hub for how the shooting was framed in real time — Level 3 Shot in the Heart X catalog, TV witness compilations, wrong-guy and confession framings, media amplification, and narratives that appeared then receded.

title: Shot in the Heart — Early Claims vs. What Video Shows
description: Catalog of 35+ X posts and TV-witness clips claiming Charlie Kirk was shot in the heart or chest on Sep 10 2025; embeds the charlie_ward77 TV clip with transcript; contrasts with no visible chest bullet hole or chest blood; all URLs clickable; claims attributed only.

title: Cause of Death
description: What citizen investigators concluded as of July 2026 killed Charlie Kirk: not the .30-06, but an exploding mic — and possibly electrocution.

title: Electrocution — The B-Field Resonant Cascade Hypothesis
description: Unverified citizen hypothesis that a pulsed electromagnetic B-field cascade delivered through a corded microphone killed Charlie Kirk.

title: The BFRC Hypothesis (Full Text)
description: Complete unedited text of the B-Field Resonant Cascade hypothesis posted by @wtshesaid72 on July 15 2026, with assessment.

title: Resonant Site Preparation
description: The BFRC claim that the UVU courtyard was prepared as a resonant site — what is claimed versus what is documented.

title: Fort Huachuca EM Expertise
description: The BFRC claim naming Fort Huachuca as the source of EM expertise, and why institutional capability is not evidence of involvement.

title: Fort Huachuca Image Search Results (Claims)
description: Stew Peters July 2026 claim that image results for Fort Huachuca-linked figures were wiped the day after broadcast — named-vs-unnamed control, misspelling probe, video embed, removal-request counterweight.

title: DIA — Search Result Claims
description: Defense Intelligence Agency thread in the Fort Huachuca image-search claim — institutional not evidentiary, Hansell/SAM-702 route, Law 2 disclosure target.

title: CENTCOM — Search Result Claims
description: US Central Command thread — Sept 8 2025 El-Sisi meeting as routine proximity, Admiral Cooper query claim, no allegation against serving officer.

title: Crane — Search Result Claims
description: NSWC Crane thread — Accurate Energetic Systems N0016425PJ538 miniaturized demolition charge contract, exploding-mic link, public contract cuts against suppression narrative.

title: Would a Rigged Mic Explain the Anomalies? (Claims)
description: Six-item convergence list arguing a rigged RODE mic with shaped charge explains bomb dogs kept away, crime scene paving, necklace residue search, no Losee shooter footage, no GSR test, and Farnsworth footage — each with mundane counterweight; no accusation against any living person.

title: Israeli Technology Inside the Investigation (Claims)
description: If Israel is a suspect, why is Israeli software authenticating the evidence? Cellebrite, BriefCam, Google Trends stacking, the Karen Read parsing precedent, Unit 8200 staffing, plus counterarguments — industry standard, US-chosen vendor, hash-verified and defense-recheckable output; marks the 'texts proven fake' and 'Cellebrite employee shot' rumors as unsupported.

title: Tyler Robinson's Phone & Devices
description: Robinson's seized electronics — reported Cellebrite extraction of his own phone (location, calls, cell-tower metadata), the fact the confession texts came off Twiggs' device not his, Discord dispute, screenshot-only presentation, and the discovery questions the defense would press.

title: Burnt Object in the SUV — RØDE Transmitter Claim
description: Project Constitution July 2026 claim that the burnt object in Kirk's SUV contains a melted female audio jack and a battery-label fragment from the RØDE transmitter; 4 images embedded; records resolution limits, screenshot-of-screenshot compression, the Wireless ME vs PRO model conflict, and that the aftermarket battery label proves no modification. UNLINKED from Level 2 TOC by request.

title: Chris Bagley
description: Profile of former UVU campus officer Christopher 'Chris' Bagley — first reported to reach the Losee Center roof, found the screwdriver, body cam reportedly died on arrival; includes the Candace Owens anonymous-officer implausibility claim with counterarguments. Witness not suspect; alive, never charged, presumed innocent.

title: Testimony
description: Sworn testimony hub for State v. Robinson — Bagley's roof and body-cam account, DNA testimony, chain of custody, screwdriver location shift, Miranda timing; distinguishes sworn testimony from non-sworn event interviews.

title: Who Testified — Hearing Witnesses
description: Roster of people who reportedly testified under oath in State v. Robinson — Bagley, Agent David Hull, forensic/DNA analysts, Lance Twiggs — and what each reportedly addressed. Roster membership is not an accusation.

title: The Judges
description: Judges hub — Tony F. Graf Jr. presiding, Robert Lunnen's August 2025 retirement and vacancy, the Dec 2025 gag order, March 2026 sealing fights, June 2026 Ballard contempt, and the attributed-unproven Google Trends claim with heavy caveats.

title: The 911 Call That Reportedly Never Came (Claims)
description: Anonymous claim relayed on the Candace Owens podcast that no 911 call came through at Timpanogos for Charlie Kirk — examined against the documented private-SUV self-transport, which would ordinarily explain it. Hospital and staff accused of nothing.

title: Suspicious
description: The things citizen investigators flag as suspicious in the Charlie Kirk case, sorted by who did them: state and local law enforcement, the FBI, an alleged Israeli nexus, US intelligence, and the defense. Attributed claims, not proven findings.

title: Suspicious by Law Enforcement
description: Eleven things citizen investigators flag as suspicious about state and local law enforcement in the Charlie Kirk case — the arrest-time paperwork, the missing surrender footage, the paved-over courtyard, the GRAMA warnings. Attributed claims, not proven findings.

title: Booking Sheet Arrest Time Contradicts the Affidavit (Claims)
description: Booking paperwork reportedly lists a 10:00pm Sept 11 arrest while a submission record lists 04:00 Sept 12. Attributed claims from citizen researchers, not proven findings.

title: No Autopsy and the Death-Certificate Questions (Claims)
description: The claim that no autopsy was performed and no death certificate exists — largely answered by Utah's standard practice of sealing medical examiner records during an active capital case.

title: Sheriff Brooksby's Resignation (Claims)
description: Investigators note that the sheriff who facilitated Tyler Robinson's surrender resigned, and pair it with UVU's president resigning the same year. An observation of adjacency, not evidence.

title: The Courtyard Paved Over Days After the Shooting (Claims)
description: A contractor says on video he was told 'the governor and the FBI said they want this done by Monday' before pavers went in four days after the shooting. Attributed claims, not proven findings.

title: The Gun Drop Location and the Search Sequence (Claims)
description: Questions about the buildings beside the rifle drop site, the unstated departure time, and evidence left out of the affidavit. Attributed claims and open questions, not proven findings.

title: Miranda at 6:25 PM Versus Sheriff Brooksby's Account (Claims)
description: Court filings reportedly show Tyler Robinson Mirandized at 6:25pm Sept 11 — before the 8:04pm tip call and 90 minutes before the Discord confession. Attributed claims, not proven findings.

title: Missing Surrender CCTV and Bodycam Footage (Claims)
description: Washington County reportedly told a public-records appeal that no bodycam footage of Tyler Robinson's surrender exists and lobby CCTV was purged at 30 days. Attributed claims, not proven findings.

title: The Unknown Woman Who Reportedly Signed the Medical Examiner Report (Claims)
description: An anonymous secondhand direct message claims Charlie Kirk's autopsy was signed by a young woman the medical examiner had never met. No source, no document — listed only so it can be checked.

title: The Claim That Robinson Did Not Surrender Voluntarily (Claims)
description: Candace Owens argues Tyler Robinson never confessed and came in out of fear rather than guilt. Attributed claims and secondhand accounts, not proven findings.

title: SUV Interior Photos With Blurred Windows (Claims)
description: Photos released by Candace Owens of the SUV interior prompted questions about fragments, charring and a blurred pane. Attributed social-media interpretations, not forensic findings.

title: UVU GRAMA Records Show Pre-Shooting Warnings (Claims)
description: A 112-page GRAMA production reportedly shows two men warning UVU about 'a plan' 14 hours before the shooting, plus a redacted message request to Charlie Kirk. Attributed claims from records.

title: Suspicious by the FBI
description: Twelve things citizen investigators flag as suspicious about the FBI in the Charlie Kirk case — the re-search that found the rifle, the witness video deletion, the banned ballistics test, the halted foreign-nexus probe. Attributed claims, not proven findings.

title: Bomb Dogs Reportedly Kept From Parts of the Scene (Claims)
description: Candace Owens has claimed the FBI did not want bomb dogs in certain places at UVU — a keystone of the rigged-microphone theory. The file's own record shows bomb dogs were called at 11:17am.

title: The Banned CBLA Bullet-Lead Test (Claims)
description: Investigators allege the FBI is reviving Comparative Bullet Lead Analysis — abandoned in 2005 — after the ATF exam came back inconclusive. An attributed inference, not a confirmed fact.

title: FBI and ATF Reportedly Withholding Discovery (Claims)
description: The claim that Robinson's defense sought a continuance because the ATF and FBI would not hand over files held since September, including DNA data and video chain of custody. Attributed and unverified.

title: Enhanced Stairwell Photos Released After Custody (Claims)
description: Baron Coleman alleges the FBI released enhanced stairwell suspect photos after Tyler Robinson was already in custody and being Mirandized. His inference from a Bates-stamped filing, not a finding.

title: Local Police Reportedly Told Not to Investigate (Claims)
description: The claim that the FBI told local law enforcement not to talk to witnesses, and that DNA testing on items recovered along the escape route was stopped. Attributed and unverified.

title: Patel Reportedly Shut Down the Kent Probe (Claims)
description: Per a Daily Mail report, FBI Director Kash Patel allegedly halted Joe Kent's counterterrorism review of a possible foreign nexus to the Kirk killing. Attributed reporting, not a proven finding.

title: The FBI Rav4 That Reportedly Flashed a Badge (Claims)
description: An unsourced list entry states an FBI Rav4 drove past Tyler Robinson right after the shooting and flashed a badge when pulled over. No source, date, or agent identification is cited.

title: Feds Directed the Re-Search That Found the Rifle (Claims)
description: The claim that hands found no gun, dogs found no gun, and the rifle turned up only after federal agents directed junior local officers to search again. Unsourced in the investigation file.

title: Selective 4K Release Versus the FBI Gag Claim (Claims)
description: Investigators argue TPUSA's October 14 tribute video used crisp 4K and drone footage from Sept 10 — footage reportedly said to be unreleasable on FBI advice. An attributed inference, not a finding.

title: Ryne Simmons Reportedly Told to Delete His 4K Video (Claims)
description: Witness Ryne Simmons says he sent his 4K footage of the shooting to the FBI and was then instructed to delete his copy, with follow-up calls. A named witness account, attributed and unverified.

title: A Surgeon Reportedly Blocked From Kirk's Room (Claims)
description: An unsourced list entry states the FBI blocked a surgeon from returning to the room with Charlie Kirk. No name, no hospital record, no source is cited anywhere in the investigation file.

title: The SUV Destroyed and the ADL Floor-Sharing Claim (Claims)
description: Two low-confidence FBI-adjacent optics items: a claim the transport SUV was chopped up and melted down, and a list entry that an FBI building shares a floor with the ADL. Both unverified.

title: Suspicious by Israel
description: Ten things citizen investigators flag as suspicious about an alleged Israeli nexus in the Charlie Kirk case — the donor texts, the foreign-registered phones, the life-insurance policy, the Hamptons meeting. Attributed claims, not proven findings.

title: The Donor Texts, 48 Hours Before (Claims)
description: Candace Owens released Sept 8, 2025 messages in which Charlie Kirk reportedly wrote that donor pressure was leaving him no choice but to leave the pro-Israel cause. TPUSA reportedly confirmed them.

title: The Egyptian Jet SU-BTT (Claims)
description: Flight trackers highlight an Egyptian-registered Falcon 7X that sat at Provo the week of the assassination. It departed five hours before the shooting — which places it hundreds of miles away.

title: The Security Chief's Reported Netanyahu-Detail Background (Claims)
description: Anonymous accounts claim a man on Charlie Kirk's security detail previously protected Benjamin Netanyahu, based on a side-by-side photo comparison. The posts do not even agree on who the man is.

title: The GGLF 2023 LLC Life-Insurance Policy (Claims)
description: Researchers claim a policy on Charlie Kirk was taken out right after he questioned October 7. The Wyoming filing offered as proof shows the LLC was formed five months before October 7.

title: Operation Grim Beeper as a Capability Argument (Claims)
description: The 2024 Hezbollah pager operation is invoked to argue a rigged-device killing is possible. It is a capability argument, not evidence — it proves what could be done, not what was done.

title: The Hamptons Intervention and the Death Question (Claims)
description: Charlie Kirk reportedly described an August 2025 Hamptons gathering as an intervention. Seth Dillon denied any angry confrontation. Erika Kirk reportedly recalled a donor asking what would happen if he died.

title: Sixteen Israeli-Registered Phones at UVU (Claims)
description: An analyst reportedly claims raw GPS data put 44 foreign-registered phones near Charlie Kirk on Sept 10, 2025, including 16 Israeli. The dataset has never been published. Unverified.

title: \"It Was Supposed to Be You\" (Claims)
description: Candace Owens reportedly attributes to Andrew Kolvet the words 'it was supposed to be you' and a claim she and Tucker Carlson were on a list. No primary source exists in the investigation file.

title: A Whistleblower's TPUSA Recruitment Claim (Claims)
description: A former TPUSA representative alleges he was questioned about Israel at a bar during training and told by two colleagues they were with Israeli intelligence. Single-source, no names, no documents.

title: Four of Those Phones Later at the Islamabad Serena (Claims)
description: An analyst reportedly claims four of the alleged UVU Israeli-registered devices pinged at the Islamabad Serena during US-Iran talks in April 2026. The circulated graphic was admitted to be a recreation.

title: Suspicious by US Intel
description: Twelve things citizen investigators flag as suspicious about US intelligence and defense-intelligence proximity to the Charlie Kirk case — Fort Huachuca, SAM-702, the HADES aircraft, the halted foreign-nexus probe. Attributed claims, not proven findings.

title: Clock Tower X FARA Filing and VFX LA (Claims)
sidebar_label: Clock Tower X and VFX LA
description: Two post-assassination influence-operation threads: a FARA registration filed eight days later, and a claim that TPUSA staff met a deepfake vendor. The file itself flags both as unconfirmed.

title: Counter-UAS Gear Tested at Provo, Sept 4-10 (Claims)
description: An OSINT researcher reports anonymous claims that anti-drone gear was tested at Provo Airport in the week ending with Charlie Kirk's death. Uncorroborated by the author's own admission.

title: Drones Over the Venue and the Butler Comparison (Claims)
description: Researchers say they identified drones in phone footage from UVU and link them to the Butler attack on Trump. Compressed video is a notorious source of false drone sightings.

title: The Fort Huachuca JTF Meeting (Claims)
description: A whistleblower says he walked into a closed high-clearance meeting at Fort Huachuca the day before Charlie Kirk was killed. Attributed claims, not proven findings.

title: Fort Huachuca Image Results Reportedly Wiped (Claims)
description: Stew Peters says Google image results for everyone his show named vanished the next day. With no archived before-state the claim is unfalsifiable, and an ordinary explanation fits exactly.

title: Joe Kent's NCTC Foreign-Nexus Probe Halted (Claims)
description: Reporting says NCTC Director Joe Kent opened a foreign-interference inquiry into the Charlie Kirk killing and that it was shut down. Attributed claims, not proven findings.

title: N1098L: The HADES Aircraft's Low Passes (Claims)
description: Citizen trackers say an Army-linked ISR jet made two low slow passes near UVU on Sept 10, 2025. The altitude figures contradict each other inside the source. Attributed claims.

title: N560TW's Sept 10 Scottsdale-Provo Turnaround (Claims)
description: Candace Owens flagged a donor-linked Cessna Citation that landed at Provo the morning of Sept 10, 2025 and left again. The pattern is an ordinary light-jet day trip.

title: NSA Bumblehive Could Unmask the Foreign Phones (Claims)
description: Tony Seruga claims NSA's Utah Data Center could identify the foreign-registered phones he says were near Charlie Kirk at UVU. The underlying dataset has never been published.

title: Palantir Reportedly Linked to the Gun-Recovery Land (Claims)
description: An unsourced line in a working list claims Palantir owned the land where the rifle was recovered. The same list contradicts it, misspells the company, and cites no record.

title: Provo Airport Badge Access List Modified 9/11/25 (Claims)
description: A one-line entry in the investigation file notes the Provo Airport badge access list was reportedly modified the day after the assassination. No document has been produced.

title: SAM-702 to Fort Huachuca With Hansell Aboard (Claims)
description: Citizen trackers say a USAF VIP jet carrying the Pentagon's top intelligence official sat at Fort Huachuca and left the day before Charlie Kirk was killed. Attributed claims, not proven findings.

title: Suspicious by Defense Attorneys
description: Nine things citizen investigators flag as suspicious about Tyler Robinson's legal defense — counsel selection, the unchallenged gag order, the Miranda exhibit, the jailhouse informant. Attributed questions, not accusations of misconduct.

title: Baron Coleman Says Robinson Is in Grave Danger (Claims)
description: Baron Coleman says he is 100% convinced Tyler Robinson was framed and is in grave danger of being killed. He offers no evidence of any threat, and Robinson is held in isolation. Attributed opinion.

title: Defense Acquiescence to the Sua Sponte Gag Order (Claims)
description: Critics ask why defense counsel never challenged Judge Tony Graf's own-motion publicity order. Trial-publicity orders in capital cases are routine and usually protect the defendant. Attributed claims only.

title: The Jailhouse Informant Transport Order (Claims)
description: A transport order for inmate Jaxson Thomas Fox is read as evidence of a jailhouse snitch being positioned. A transport order is a ministerial docket entry that reveals nothing about testimony.

title: Kent's Halted Probe as Defense Ammunition (Claims)
description: Daily Mail reporting says officials worried Joe Kent's foreign-interference probe could give Robinson's lawyers a multi-suspect argument. Critics ask why counsel has not publicly demanded it.

title: The Miranda Timing Came From a Defense Filing (Claims)
description: Baron Coleman's 6:25 PM Miranda claim comes from a defense motion quoting a Bates-stamped exhibit. Critics ask why counsel has not made it a public suppression fight. Attributed claims, not findings.

title: Missing Surrender Video and Discovery Volume (Claims)
description: Baron Coleman says surrender footage was refused, then reported deleted under a 30-day policy, while pleadings suggest it exists. His own account describes an active discovery fight. Attributed claims.

title: Kathryn Nester and the Google Trends Spike (Claims)
description: An anonymous post reads a December 2024 Google Trends graph as proof Kathryn Nester was pre-selected from abroad. Trends cannot report IP addresses, and the claim's legal premise is impossible.

title: Parents Reportedly Declined High-End Attorneys (Claims)
description: An unsourced list item says Tyler Robinson's parents declined high-end attorneys; a second says the prosecutor blocked volunteers. The two claims contradict each other and one is legally impossible.

title: Not-Guilty Plea Versus the Confession Narrative (Claims)
description: Candace Owens argues no sworn written confession exists and Robinson pleaded not guilty. Critics ask why the defense has not publicly rebutted the confession narrative. Attributed claims, not findings.

title: Suspicious About the Trial and Legally
description: Twelve things citizen investigators flag as suspicious about how the Charlie Kirk case is being adjudicated — closed hearings, the publicity order, the extraction disclaimer, the death notice. Attributed claims, not proven findings.

title: Hearings Closed, No Transcripts Released (Claims)
description: Citizen media report hearings closed to press with no transcripts issued. Attributed claims; closure is lawful and a news coalition is already litigating access — but the objection limits every other claim.

title: The Death Notice Filed Six Days After the Shooting (Claims)
description: Commentators read a capital notice filed six days after the shooting as a rush to judgment. Attributed claims; early capital notice is standard and protects the defendant.

title: Defense Denied Access to Destructive Ballistics Testing (Claims)
description: A defense motion reports requests to photograph and attend testing of the autopsy bullet fragment were denied. Attributed claims; motion practice working as designed, not failing.

title: The 600,000-Document Dump Against Continuance Opposition (Claims)
description: Posts say the State buried the defense in documents while opposing a continuance. Attributed claims; the two circulating figures differ by an order of magnitude and neither is sourced to a filing.

title: The Jailhouse Informant Transported Before a Closed Hearing (Claims)
description: Citizen media infer a jailhouse informant from a transport order they concede is their 'only logical explanation.' Attributed claims; transport orders are ministerial and issue by the dozen.

title: The Judges' Google Trends Claims (Claims)
description: A thread reads Google Trends as showing Israeli IP searches for two Utah judges before the assassination. Trends cannot report IP addresses, and the judicial timeline is public and mundane.

title: Kennington Released With No Bail, Charges Dropped (Claims)
description: Posts read a same-day release and later dismissal as a cover-up. Attributed claims; every element has an ordinary reading under Utah open-carry and bail practice.

title: The Sua Sponte Publicity Order's Breadth (Claims)
description: Commentators call a publicity order the court reportedly issued on its own motion a First Amendment sledgehammer. Attributed claims; sua sponte entry is well-established doctrine.

title: The Prosecution Disclaimed the Extraction Evidence (Claims)
description: A State filing reportedly says it will not offer forensic-extraction evidence — the source of the entire confession narrative. Attributed claims; most likely narrow lawyering, not a concession.

title: The Prosecution Never Called the Roof Eyewitness (Claims)
description: Investigators say the only person who filmed the roof figure was never called, and that DNA testing on recovered items stopped. Attributed claims; a prelim calls minimum witnesses by design.

title: Erika Kirk-Linked Lawyers Opposed the Continuance (Claims)
description: Commentators question why victim-side counsel opposed the defense's continuance. Attributed claims; opposing delay is the exercise of a statutory victims' right, not interference.

title: Prosecutors Accused of Withholding the Rooftop Video (Claims)
description: A commentator back-calculates from a trimmed clip that the shot was impossible. Attributed claims; every input is an uncorroborated estimate and the defense has the full files.

title: Suspicious Peripheral to TPUSA
description: Twelve things citizen investigators flag as suspicious around Turning Point USA — the DOGE audit, the succession, the money, the SD cards. Attributed claims, not proven findings.

title: The Cease-and-Desist and the Drone Contradiction (Claims)
description: Candace Owens says a TPUSA cease-and-desist letter conceded a media drone flew before the UVU event. Attributed claims from her account; the letter itself is not in the file.

title: Cooper Brown and the Silenced Witness (Claims)
description: Viral clips assign the name Cooper Brown three incompatible roles — silencer, silenced, and bystander volunteer. The investigation file concedes the identifications have shifted.

title: The DOGE Audit and Bowyer's Removal Timing (Claims)
description: George Webb claims Tyler Bowyer was moved out over a refused DOGE audit eight days before the assassination. The investigation file contradicts itself on this point.

title: The Disputed CEO Appointment and the Aspen Donors (Claims)
description: Candace Owens cites unnamed Aspen Summit donors who say Charlie Kirk never made the recorded statement appointing Erika Kirk CEO. Unnamed hearsay; no donor is identified.

title: The Senior Executive Who Collected the SD Cards (Claims)
description: Candace Owens says a top TPUSA executive who never works campus stops was at UVU and took the camera SD cards. Attributed claim; no attendance record supports it.

title: Dan Flood Scrubbed From the TPUSA Site (Claims)
description: Web archives reportedly show Dan Flood listed as a TPUSA officer on Sept 1, 2025 and gone afterward. The archive comparison is checkable; the rest of the thread is not, and is omitted.

title: The Insider-Payment Chart and the Missing 990s (Claims)
description: A viral thread alleges $15.2M in insider-directed TPUSA payments and missing 990s. No filing, page number, or year is cited, and its headline figure rests on an arithmetic error.

title: The Yrefy Sponsor Rep at the Table Handoff (Claims)
description: The file says a man in post-shooting imagery 'looks a lot like' a TPUSA sponsor representative from Yrefy. It is a resemblance claim and nothing more, and no conduct is alleged.

title: The Alleged Secret $5M Employee Stock Fund (Claims)
description: A former TPUSA representative claims Charlie Kirk found a hidden $5M stock fund with zero employees. One ex-staffer, no document, no 990 line item, and his own framing is inference.

title: The Whistleblower's Pre-Death CEO Claim (Claims)
description: An anonymous TPUSA insider claim relayed through two accounts alleges Erika Kirk assumed CEO duties before the death and ordered security to stand down. No document, no named source.

title: The COO's Alleged Call to 'Pierre Du Pont' (Claims)
description: Candace Owens alleges TPUSA's new COO took a call after the shooting with someone she believes is 'Pierre Du Pont.' No call record, no confirmed identity, no documented billion dollars.

title: Replacement on Fox Within Hours (Claims)
description: The file claims Charlie Kirk's replacement announced himself on Fox within hours of the death. Across 10,000+ lines the person is never named and the segment is never linked.

title: Suspicious Hospital, Autopsy and Medical
description: Ten things citizen investigators flag as suspicious about the hospital, the transport, the medical examiner and the autopsy in the Charlie Kirk case. Attributed claims, not proven findings.

title: The Body Flown Out, and No Burial Site (Claims)
description: Two one-line items ask why the body was flown out and why there is no burial site. The investigation file contains its own rebuttal: there was a public memorial, and a grave's location is family privacy.

title: Charred Material and Fragments in the SUV (Claims)
description: Photos of the transport SUV are read by some analysts as showing charred material and fragments. Nothing has been recovered, identified, or lab-tested. Reported claims only.

title: Chest Wound Accounts Versus the Neck Wound (Claims)
description: Early witnesses described a chest or heart wound when the wound was in the neck. Eyewitness psychology explains this well — and the witnesses are private people who did nothing but talk to a reporter.

title: The Empty ER and Hospital Personnel Changes (Claims)
description: Claims of a cleared ER and suspicious new hospital staff. The file contradicts itself three lines apart about a named CEO, and retracts one appointment claim inside the same section.

title: The Necklace, Cavitation and Residue (Claims)
description: Two leading theories explain the snapped necklace — one requires a bullet, one requires no bullet. They directly refute each other, and one rests on a well-known physics error.

title: No On-Site Ambulance and the Scoop-and-Run SUV (Claims)
description: Kirk was carried to the hospital by his own detail in an SUV, with no ambulance. An anonymous claim says a 911 call never reached the hospital. Hospitals do not receive 911 calls.

title: The OR Timeline Versus the Jerusalem Post (Claims)
description: A reconstructed surgical timeline is said to end after the Jerusalem Post announced Kirk's death. Neither time is sourced, and the file elsewhere says he was DOA — which removes the OR window entirely.

title: The Timpanogos Surgeon Search-Trends Frenzy (Claims)
description: A post claims Israeli IPs searched seven Timpanogos doctors before the shooting. Google Trends cannot report IP addresses at all. Seven living physicians are implicated by a chart that cannot say this.

title: Was a Nearer Level II Trauma Center Bypassed? (Claims)
description: A post argues Kirk was driven past a closer Level II trauma center to a Level IV hospital. The investigation file contradicts itself on the distances, and the conspiracy-friendly version is the unsourced one.

title: The Wound Trajectory Entrance-Exit Dispute (Claims)
description: Online analysts dispute the official wound account — some say no exit, some say the neck IS the exit, some say no bullet. The theories cancel each other out. No pathologist endorses any of them.

title: Suspicious at UVU on 9/10
description: Twelve things citizen investigators flag as suspicious about the venue, the setup, the equipment and the people at Utah Valley University on September 10, 2025. Attributed claims, not proven findings.

title: The Acoustics Claim: Shot Came From the South (Claims)
description: An unnamed engineer reportedly concluded the shot came from the south. It is one of three mutually incompatible shot origins the investigation file asserts simultaneously.

title: The All-Black Man the Construction Worker Met (Claims)
description: Electrician Dylan Hope reportedly described a man in all black right after the shooting. Early eyewitness and dispatch descriptions in mass-panic events are famously unreliable.

title: The Burnt Object in the SUV (Claims)
description: Project Constitution argues a charred mass in Kirk's SUV contains RØDE transmitter parts. The theory cannot resolve which RØDE model it is matching, and nothing has been tested.

title: Butch Hibbs and Backstage Access (Claims)
description: An anonymous claim treats a pastor's brother having backstage access as suspicious. The innocent explanation is the one the file states and then discards. This page is a debunk.

title: The Canon XA55 Audio and a Third Boom (Claims)
description: An unsourced analysis claims a broadcast camera's uncompressed audio resolves three events including a detonation at the tent. Its own 120m rifle estimate corroborates the rooftop account.

title: The Claim a Group Blocked University Security (Claims)
description: A one-line unsourced claim that a group blocked university security, which the investigation file itself immediately questions in-line with the words 'Is that true'.

title: The Mic Mounted Under the Shirt, Not Over (Claims)
description: The investigation file asks why Kirk's wireless mic was reportedly placed under his shirt with a furry windscreen attached. Under-shirt mounting is standard broadcast practice.

title: Rick Cutler, the Arm-Pull and the Hand Device (Claims)
description: A retired officer near the stage is said to have worn a device on his hand. Everything described is ordinary close-protection work. The file's own compiler refused to publish the worst version.

title: Tyler Robinson's Clothing Changes That Day (Claims)
description: Tyler Robinson reportedly changed clothes several times on September 10 and was arrested in his morning outfit. A limp appears and vanishes. He is charged, not convicted.

title: The Executive Who Removed the Camera SD Card (Claims)
description: An unnamed executive reportedly removed a camera SD card after the shooting. Candace Owens has separately accused Terryl Farnsworth. The file does not establish they are the same person.

title: Six Campus Officers and No Ambulance Staged (Claims)
description: UVU reportedly assigned six campus officers and staged no ambulance for a 3,000-person outdoor event. Documented via a records request; negligence is the ordinary explanation.

title: Stairs Guy and the Backpack That Doesn't Fit (Claims)
description: Investigators argue the stairwell figure is a different person because the backpack and hat don't match and a Mauser 98 won't fit. The file's own backpack timeline contradicts itself.

title: Suspicious Within Five Minutes
description: Everything citizen investigators flag as suspicious inside the ten minutes from 12:18:30 to 12:28:30 PM MT on September 10, 2025 — five minutes either side of the shot. Attributed claims, not proven findings.

title: Farnsworth Already Filming From the Rock Pile (Claims)
description: Terryl Farnsworth was reportedly filming from a rock pile 27 seconds after the shot and said 'He's dead' at 12:24:36. Critics call it foreknowledge; the ordinary reading is a bystander's reaction.

title: Three Minutes to the First Police Report (Claims)
description: Police audio reportedly carries the first report of the shooting at 12:26 — after the SUV had already left. Two and a half minutes to a radio channel is fast, not slow.

title: McCoy Reportedly Phones His Wife as the Shot Lands (Claims)
description: A reconstruction places Mikey McCoy calling his wife at 12:23:30, the same second as the shot. No phone record or source supports the timestamp — it is an inference from footage.

title: McCoy Alone in Frame, Neff Nowhere (Claims)
description: Blake Neff has said he witnessed McCoy's calls, yet at 12:24:02 McCoy is reportedly alone in frame. This is a timeline-reconstruction question, not an accusation, and every premise is soft.

title: The N1098L Low Pass at 12:24 (Claims)
description: A HADES-program aircraft reportedly made a 335-foot low pass near UVU at 12:24, thirty seconds after the shot. An aircraft cannot react in thirty seconds, and the file's own numbers conflict.

title: The Rifle Wrapped, Placed on the Roof, the Descent (Claims)
description: A compiled timeline says the rifle was set on the Losee roof at 12:24 — yet the Mauser was recovered in the woods. The tension resolves if the item was set down only to free both hands.

title: The 12:22 Roof Run and the Vanishing Limp (Claims)
description: A compiled timeline says the roof figure limped all morning, then ran without a noticeable limp at 12:22. The entry carries no source and rests on an observer impression.

title: The Claim the Runner Started Before the Shot (Claims)
description: An unattributed passage back-extrapolates a rooftop runner's start to 12:23:28, two seconds before the shot. The arithmetic assumes constant velocity from a standing start, which is wrong.

title: The Shirt Pulled at the Instant of the Shot (Claims)
description: Exploding-mic proponents read a 4K close-up as showing an object driven up under Kirk's shirt. The theory's own cited explosives expert says the mechanism would move the mic the other way.

title: The Shot Time: 12:23 or 12:27? (Claims)
description: The investigation file records two shot times, 12:23 and 12:27, and asks itself which is correct. The 12:27 figure is never sourced and everything else in the file settles on 12:23:30.

title: Suspicious About Cause of Death
description: Eleven things citizen investigators find suspicious about the official rifle-shot account of how Charlie Kirk died — the energy mismatch, the shirt sequence, the acoustics, the ATF fragment. Attributed claims, not proven findings.

title: The Acoustics Point South, Not to Losee (Claims)
description: An unnamed acoustics engineer reportedly places the shot South, above the Teachers' balcony. The site's own Canon audio analysis puts the rifle at ~120m — the Losee roof. Both cannot be right.

title: The ATF Fragment Shows Only Class Characteristics (Claims)
description: The ATF's Sept 17, 2025 lab report reportedly could neither identify nor exclude Robinson's Mauser 98 as the source of the recovered jacket fragment — a documented gap, widely overclaimed by both camps.

title: The Burnt Object as a RODE Transmitter (Claims)
description: Proponents read a melted audio jack and a warning symbol into a charred mass photographed in the SUV. The imagery cannot support the identification, and the theory cannot agree on the model.

title: The Canon XA55 Audio and a Stage Detonation (Claims)
description: An analysis of uncompressed Canon XA55 audio claims three low-frequency arrivals including a detonation at the tent. Reflections off campus buildings produce exactly the same pattern.

title: The Rick Cutler Hand-Device Claim (Claims)
description: Recorded only as a specimen of what is circulating. Anonymous accounts read a dark object in a named private citizen's hand as a trigger. This site does not advance the claim.

title: Electrocution as Trigger and Redundancy (Claims)
description: A strand of theory holds electricity was part of the mechanism. No agency reported any electrical injury, and electrical injury leaves pathology nobody has described. This is the weakest material on the site.

title: No Blood Spray, No Exit Wound (Claims)
description: The most-repeated suspicion: a .30-06 should have blown through the neck and sprayed blood. The counterargument includes a physics error that undercuts the analysts making it.

title: Is the Front Neck Wound an Entrance or an Exit? (Claims)
description: Proponents argue the front neck wound is an exit — and contradict each other completely about where the shot came from. None has produced medical documentation.

title: The Mic Placed Under, Not Over, the Shirt (Claims)
description: The RODE transmitter was reportedly worn under Kirk's shirt against his torso — the precondition every contact-device theory needs. Under-shirt placement is also routine professional practice.

title: The Snapped Necklace and the Residue Search (Claims)
description: Both camps treat the snapped chain as a clue. One cited expert says cavitation from a bullet explains it; another says a PETN charge in the pendant did. Both cannot be right.

title: The Shirt and Necklace Move Before the Blood (Claims)
description: Analysts argue the shirt balloons and the chain snaps several frames before blood appears. Hydrostatic cavitation predicts exactly that, and 30fps cannot resolve a millisecond event.
